response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js/DLang developer whose C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far below the mandatory 5–10 years. He satisfies neither qualifying path: he lacks capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain experience, and his one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) involved general fintech tooling unrelated to trading systems or financial instruments. With no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work, SQL Server/Oracle usage, or relevant domain knowledge, the candidate does not meet the core requirements of this role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) demonstrated consistently across multiple roles
Broad technology exposure including Java, SQL, Docker, and scripting languages shows general engineering adaptability
Active conference talks and full-day workshops demonstrate solid verbal and written communication ability
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
Zero capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge present anywhere in the resume
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage, Windows-platform development, or financial messaging frameworks (Summit, Kafka, MQ)

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain
